title: Meditating on psychedelics. A randomized placebo-controlled study of DMT and harmine in a mindfulness retreat.

assets.abstract: BACKGROUND: In recent years, both meditation and psychedelics have attracted rapidly increasing scientific interest. While the current state of evidence suggests the promising potential of psychedelics, such as psilocybin, to enhance meditative training, it remains equivocal whether these effects are specifically bound to psilocybin or if other classical psychedelics might show synergistic effects with meditation practice. One particularly promising candidate is  In recent years, both meditation and psychedelics have attracted rapidly increasing scientific interest. While the current state of evidence suggests the promising potential of psychedelics, such as psilocybin, to enhance meditative training, it remains equivocal whether these effects are specifically bound to psilocybin or if other classical psychedelics might show synergistic effects with meditation practice. One particularly promising candidate is  N,N -dimethyltryptamine (DMT), an active ingredient of ayahuasca. AIM: This

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Meditating on psychedelics — DMT and harmine in meditation: bridging pharmacological and contemplative pathways to nondual states

evaluation: Score 4: Good bridge (4 indicators) — Placebo-controlled study of DMT in meditation context, directly bridging pharmacological and contemplative approaches to altered states.
